Ajay MaldonaldoBiographical informationOccupation:ChefSpecies:HumanStatus:DeceasedCause of death:Truck crashed into theCrash PitGender:MaleHair color:Dark brownShow informationVoiced by:Roger Craig SmithAppears in:The Best Burger in the World Ajay Maldonaldowas the head chef of the Grill 'Em Up Truck. He is accredited with making "the best burger in the world": theUlti-Meatum. He makes his first and only appearance in the episode "The Best Burger in the World." He was killed after the truck with him and his co-worker inside rolled into theCrash Pitand blew up.

characters.name: Ancient Order of the VHS
characters.description: You can help clean up this page by correcting spelling and grammar, removing factual errors and rewriting sections to ensure they are clear and concise, and moving some elements when appropriate. Ancient Order of the VHSBiographical informationOccupation:Guardians of VHSSpecies:HumansGender:MalesShow informationFirst appearance:The Last Laserdisc Player TheAncient Order of the VHSare the main antagonists of theSeason FourepisodeThe Last Laserdisc Player. They are a group of men whose job it is to protect the VHS. The leaders,SP and LP, first appear in the library, tellingMordecai,Rigby,Muscle ManandHi-Five Ghostthat they should use VHS instead of laserdisc players. They appear again in the basement, preventing the Librarian from giving Mordecai and Rigby the last laserdisc player, because they aren't using VHS. They are about to destroy the laserdisc player, when theGuardians of Obsolete Formatsstop them. They eventually get defeated by the Guardians and by theLibrarian, whose true form is a supernatural crystalline creature known as the Laserdisc Guardian. For some reason, almost every single member has some form of facial hair and a variety of old fashioned haircuts.

characters.name: Angus
characters.description: You can help clean up this page by correcting spelling and grammar, removing factual errors and rewriting sections to ensure they are clear and concise, and moving some elements when appropriate. AngusBiographical informationOccupation:Opposition Leader (Australian Politics)Species:HumanGender:MaleShow informationVoiced by:Robin Atkin Downes(grumbles)Roger Craig Smith(punch the Prime Minister line)Appears in:Mordecai and Rigby Down Under  Angus is a mean-spirited, vengeful man who holds a grudge against the Australian Prime Minister for beating him in the election. He seems to be mentally unstable as he mostly shouts his words and stabs a can of soda with a knife to drink out of it.

characters.name: Animal Spirits
characters.description: Animal SpiritsBiographical informationHome:Ancient Animal Burial GroundSpecies:MooseBearsOwlsWolvesGoatsBeaversPiranhasGender:Male(Moose)Hair color:Light GreenShow informationVoiced by:Fred TatascioreAppears in:Blind Trust TheAnimal Spiritsappeared as the main antagonists in the episodeBlind Trustwhere they were discovered in an ancient animal burial ground. The moose serves as their leader. AfterPopstakes the three of them up to the top of a mountain to testBensonon his trust forMordecaiandRigby,Mordecaijokes about howBensonwas blinded by nature.Bensonthen walks off and tells the two of them he is going to find his way down the mountain himself, despite being unable to see. Mordecai and Rigby still follow him, though, warning him about rocks in the way.

characters.name: Announcer Bot
characters.description: Announcer BotBiographical informationHome:JapanSpecies:RobotGender:MaleShow informationVoiced by:Sam MarinAppears in:Fool Me Twice Announcer Botis a minor character inRegular Showwho first appeared in the episode "Fool Me Twice". He is the co host of a Japanese TV game show calledFool Me Once, Shame on You, Fool Me Twice, I Punch Your Face!of the host, Game MasterMitsuru Shinehara. Announcer Bot states that the show is looking for American contestants. It also says that at 4:30 in the afternoon, a telephone number will flash onscreen and the first person to dial the number will be chosen to participate on the show.MordecaiandRigbysay that they will attempt to enter the contest. And when they do, Mordecai and Rigby along withBensonget teleported to Japan. They end up at the game show and participate in it and managed to win. When they get to pick the prizes the Announcer-Bot comes in and tells the three that they're the very first contestants to ever beat the Baka Blitz without being punched or dying. Because of this, Announcer-Bot says that he "failed." Announcer-Bot says sorry to his master (Shinehara) until his parts all break apart and he dies.

Because of this, Announcer-Bot says that he "failed." Announcer-Bot says sorry to his master (Shinehara) until his parts all break apart and he dies.

characters.name: Anti-Pops
characters.description: Anti-PopsBiographical informationHome:Anti-Pops' Space StationSpecies:LollilanderStatus:DeceasedCause of death:Hugged by his brother while flying into the sunGender:MaleRelatives:Pops'(brother)'Show informationVoiced by:Robert EnglundFirst appearance:The Dream WarriorAppearances:Space EscapeThe Key to the UniverseNo Train No GainKill 'Em with KindnessA Regular Epic Final Battle Malum Kranus, also known asAnti-Pops, is the main antagonist ofSeason Eight. He is the last and most powerful villain, and he made his first debut in the episode "The Dream Warrior,” and later made a full appearance in "Space Escape.” He looks similar toPops, having a darker appearance. He is Pops' illegitimate brother, and both siblings were born on their home planetLolliland. He and Pops are fated to battle each other for the fate of the universe every 14 billion years, but every single time, the duel ends in a stalemate and their perfectly-balanced powers cause the universe to reset. A complete opposite of Pops, Anti-Pops is a rude, tyrannical, homicidal, ruthless, destructive, malevolent, and genocidal entity bent on destroying all existences in the universe. He is also extremely picky about food and beverages as he often tries to rate the lowest rank (often one star) for any kind of online food and beverage service, which displeases him. On one occasion, he is enraged to know that he cannot post a negative star for bad service. Anti-Pops acts disrespectfully towards anyone and does not even care about the life of any existing being, undoing his anger by erasing things or people. He is always angry and yells at others as he happens to his soldiers. The only thing that makes him happy and laugh is when he murders others for fun. After being defeated by Pops due to an interference of time-traveling Mordecai and Rigby, Anti-Pops was afraid of dying at the same time that he and his brother die together to the sun during his brother's sacrifice to stop Anti-Pops' terror. But he did show remorse for his past actions and gave his brother a hug to atone them.

characters.description: ArmandoBiographical informationOccupation:Factory workerSpecies:HumanGender:MaleShow informationAppears in:Firework Run Armando is a character who appears as a minor antagonist  in the episodeFirework Run.He is a friend ofHectorand the one in charge of putting gunpowder inside theSouth of the Line Chile Rellenos. Armando wears a brown bathrobe and slippers, beard, mustache and black hair, it is also very long, he seems dirty and he is wearing a white T-shirt (seems muscular), and gray underwear and socks.

characters.name: Baby Ducks
characters.description: Baby DucksBiographical informationSpecies:DucksStatus:AliveGender:MaleHair color:YellowRelatives:Mother Duck(mother)Andy(brother)Show informationVoiced by:J.G. Quintel(Hyperduck)William SalyersSam MarinMark HamillRoger Craig Smith(s6)First appearance:A Bunch of Baby DucksAppearances:See list. TheBaby Ducksare minor characters inRegular Show. They made their debut in theSeason Twoepisode "A Bunch of Baby Ducks". In "A Bunch of Baby Ducks", they are shown living by the park fountain untilMordecaiandRigbyfind them, yet discovered byBenson, who briefly mocks Mordecai and Rigby when they tell him that the ducks imprinted on them, before asserting that they have to clean up the fountain and get rid of the baby ducks. At first, they didn't mind getting rid of them, but later, they wanted to keep them. Mordecai and Rigby take them to a pet shelter but after the baby ducks karate chop a dog, and a table with a glass tank containing a snake, who they knocked out cold, they didn't get accepted.

characters.name: Barry & Jones
characters.description: You can help clean up this page by correcting spelling and grammar, removing factual errors and rewriting sections to ensure they are clear and concise, and moving some elements when appropriate. Barry & JonesBiographical informationHome:UnknownOccupation:AstronautsSpecies:HumanStatus:AliveGender:MaleHair color:Jones: BlackBarry: BrownRelatives:UnknownShow informationVoiced by:Jones:Mark HamillBarry:Steve BlumFirst appearance:Grilled Cheese DeluxeAppearances:The Thanksgiving SpecialBrilliant Century Duck CrisisVIP Members Only Barry:Steve Blum Barry & Jones are shown to be very friendly to Mordecai and Rigby when they overheard that they were astronauts, unaware of the fact that they were lying about it. They are extremely social and were fond of the duo's fabricated stories when they were touring the compound. However, as soon as the anti-matter nucleus became unstable, they weren't hesitant (Jones in particular) on sacrificing Rigby to prevent catastrophic losses of innocent lives. When the duo finally dropped their facade, the astronauts kicked them out of the compound and had to drive the duo back to Pop's House and threatened violence should they ever pretend to be astronauts again, severing whatever friendship they had with Mordecai and Rigby.

characters.name: Benson
characters.description: Benson DunwoodyBiographical informationHome:The HouseApartment 1635(formerly)Space Tree(formerly)Occupation:Park ManagerDrummer (formerly)Space Ranger (formerly)Builder (formerly)Groundskeeper (formerly)Species:Gumball machineStatus:AliveGender:MaleRelatives:Unnamed parentsUnnamed sisterPam(wife)Romance(s):Ashley (formerly)Monica (formerly)Patricia (formerly)Lisa (formerly)Audrey(formerly)Natalie(formerly)Roxy(formerly)Show informationVoiced by:Sam MarinFirst appearance:"The Pilot" (unofficial)"The Power" (official)Appearances:See list  Benson Dunwoody[1]is one of the seven main characters ofRegular Show. He is a gumball machine, and was originally known asThe Park's manager in the entire series until the series finale, after the death ofMr. Maellard, after which he presumably became the owner. Benson is easily the most short-tempered character of all. His short temper is usually contributed by the lack of productivity by Mordecai and Rigby, as they always prefer to slack off and play video games rather than doing their jobs. Benson often threatens to fire Mordecai and Rigby if they don't finish their work or do a simple task such as buying tortillas or going to a Karaoke Bar. Whenever they destroy park property or slack off, Benson loses his temper, turning varying shades of red, and often ends up yelling at Mordecai and Rigby. Although this could be because he wants to push them to do better and to learn from their mistakes. Aside from his short fuse, Benson is usually calm and, in fact, is friendly and social, even with Mordecai and Rigby. Whenever Benson is in a good mood, he'll reward his employees with time off, usually in the form of nightly entertainment or dining out. He even admits to Mordecai and Rigby he doesn’t hate them, just their actions, because it makes him look bad as their boss.

characters.name: Bert Coleman
characters.description: You can help clean up this page by correcting spelling and grammar, removing factual errors and rewriting sections to ensure they are clear and concise, and moving some elements when appropriate. Bert ColemanBiographical informationOccupation:Host ofExpert or Liar(and numerous other game shows)Species:HumanGender:MaleHair color:OrangeShow informationVoiced by:Troy BakerAppears in:Expert or Liar  Bert is known for being sadistic and egotistical, antagonizing the contestants of his game show to great lengths on national television. He even goes as far as having his co-workers punching them and calling them names in order to humiliate them for ratings (to which he said were really good). He tried his hardest to prevent Rigby from winning Expert or Liar and laughed in his face when he thought he got the last question wrong.

characters.name: Blondes
characters.description: You can help clean up this page by correcting spelling and grammar, removing factual errors and rewriting sections to ensure they are clear and concise, and moving some elements when appropriate. BlondesBiographical informationSpecies:HumanStatus:DeceasedCause of death:Ring explosion in a room full of hydrogen peroxideGender:MaleHair color:BlondeShow informationVoiced by:Mark Hamill(leader)William SalyersRoger Craig SmithFirst appearance:Bet to Be Blonde  On the surface, the Blondes seem like good friends to hang out with if you're blonde. They understand other blonde's struggles and also share common interests with them. They also hate people with other hair colors. However, this friendly persona is secretly a trap to get other people to join their cult, which show how much they hate people with other hair colors. If the new addition betrays their pact with the blondes by not killing an outsider, the blondes act more aggressive towards them.
